data.title: U.S. prosecutors in Connecticut recover $600,000 in cryptocurrency assets obtained through a Ledger phishing scam.
data.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of Connecticut announced the recovery and forfeiture of over $600,000 in cryptocurrency after a Ledger hardware wallet user fell victim to a phishing scam in September 2025. The user was tricked into performing a so-called security check by a letter impersonating "Ledger Security and Compliance," resulting in the theft of approximately $234,000 in crypto assets. The FBI and state police traced the funds, successfully seizing approximately $600,000 in USDT and filing a civil forfeiture lawsuit, alleging that the funds originated from wire fraud and money laundering. This is reportedly one of a series of phishing attacks targeting hardware wallet users, where scammers used physical mail containing company logos, holograms, and QR codes to increase the credibility of the fraud. Similar incidents have affected companies such as Ledger and Trezor, leading to data breaches and vulnerabilities in third-party services, triggering ongoing phishing attacks. (Decrypt)

data.title: Iran will impose tolls on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister announced that Iran will impose passage fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: Restoring Inflation to 2% is Crucial
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan emphasized the importance of returning inflation to the 2% target. According to Jin10, Logan stated that achieving this goal is vital for economic stability and growth. The statement underscores the ongoing efforts by the Federal Reserve to manage inflationary pressures and maintain a balanced economic environment.

data.title: X Platform Implements Anti-Phishing Measures to Combat Crypto Scams
data.description: X Product Lead Nikita Bier announced that the platform is advancing a security mechanism to counter phishing incidents related to cryptocurrency. According to Foresight News, the new system will automatically lock accounts and initiate identity verification if a user posts crypto-related content for the first time in their account history. This measure is expected to eliminate 99% of scam attempts, particularly addressing the lack of action from Google in preventing phishing emails.

data.title: Entity Withdraws 6,328 ETH from Exchange
data.description: A single entity has withdrawn a total of 6,328 ETH from an exchange using two newly created linked wallets. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction was monitored by Onchain Lens.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Shortened U.S. Homeland Security Shutdown
data.description:
The probability of a U.S. Department of Homeland Security shutdown lasting more than 70 days has significantly decreased to 8%, according to Odaily. This marks a 45% drop in the past 24 hours, with the event contract's trading volume exceeding $1.27 million. The contract stipulates that if the shutdown, starting from February 14, 2026, lasts at least the number of days listed in the market, it will settle as 'Yes'; otherwise, it will settle as 'No'. The end date of the shutdown will be determined by the date a funding bill to restart the department is signed by the President or otherwise enacted, with mere announcements of a restart not being considered.

U.S. President Donald Trump recently announced plans to sign an order to pay all employees of the Department of Homeland Security. He also expressed gratitude to House Speaker Johnson and Thune for their efforts this week and praised congressional Republicans for making progress on the Homeland Security issue.

data.title: Two newly created wallets withdrew 6,328 ETH from Binance, worth $12.98 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, two newly created wallets withdrew 6,328 ETH from Binance, worth $12.98 million. These two wallets may belong to the same entity.

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell sharply in the short term, with WTI crude oil trading at $108.93 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell sharply in the short term, with WTI crude dropping nearly $5 to $108.93 per barrel. Reports indicate that Iran is drafting a passage agreement for the Strait of Hormuz with Oman.

data.title: Product Manager X: An automatic locking and verification mechanism will be implemented for accounts that post encrypted content for the first time.
data.description: Nikita Bier, the platform's product lead and Solana ecosystem advisor, stated that the X platform is implementing a countermeasure against cryptocurrency phishing scams: if an account posts cryptocurrency-related content for the first time in its history, the platform will automatically lock the account and require identity verification, which is expected to eliminate 99% of malicious motives.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 67,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.97% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 02, 2026, 14:45 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 67,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 67,082.851563 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.97% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Murad Mahmudov Predicts SPX6900's Market Cap Could Hit $1 Trillion Despite Portfolio Decline
data.description: Murad Mahmudov has expressed optimism that SPX6900 could achieve a market capitalization of $1 trillion, despite a significant drop in his memecoin portfolio. According to NS3.AI, Mahmudov's portfolio experienced a decline of approximately $60 million from its peak. Data from Arkham Intelligence reveals that wallets labeled 'Muststopmurad' contained around 29.964 million SPX, valued at approximately $7.79 million on Wednesday, representing about 96% of his tracked portfolio.

data.title: US bank and crypto industry executives will conduct a "small-scale" review of the stablecoin yield agreement text in order to resolve the dispute as quickly as possible.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett reported on the X platform that a small group of banking and crypto industry executives will review the text of a new stablecoin yield agreement over the next two days, aiming to resolve the lobbying dispute over stablecoin yields in the previous CLARITY bill as soon as possible. It is understood that the text will be limited to viewing by specific parties and will not be made more widely publicized. Those who visited Capitol Hill last week were able to review the text on-site but were not allowed to bring copies. Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal previously stated in an interview with Fox Business that the debate over stablecoin yields in the upcoming CLARITY bill is "very close" to reaching an agreement.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that the "US Department of Homeland Security shutdown will last more than 70 days" has plummeted to 8%, a 45% drop in 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of a Department of Homeland Security shutdown lasting more than 70 days has plummeted to 8% on Polymarket, a 45% drop in 24 hours. The contract volume for this event has exceeded $1.27 million. The contract rules state that if the Department of Homeland Security shutdown, which began on February 14, 2026, lasts for at least the number of days listed in the market (including start and end dates), it will settle as "Yes"; otherwise, it will settle as "No." The end date of the shutdown will be based on the date the President signs or otherwise enacts the appropriations bill for the reopening of the Department of Homeland Security; announcements of an impending reopening are not counted. President Trump just stated that he will soon sign an order to pay the salaries of all the admirable employees of the Department of Homeland Security. Trump also thanked House Speaker Johnson and Thune for their work this week and praised the progress made by congressional Republicans on the Department of Homeland Security issue. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Survey Reveals Preference for US Dollar Over BRICS Currencies
data.description: A recent online poll conducted among 50 participants indicates a strong preference for the US dollar over BRICS currencies for trading, holding, and investment purposes. According to NS3.AI, 47 respondents expressed their inclination towards the US dollar, while only three participants favored BRICS currencies as a means to bolster local economies. This survey highlights the continued dominance of the US dollar in global financial activities.

data.title: Fed's Logan Supports Holding Interest Rates Steady at Recent FOMC Meeting
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan has expressed support for the decision to maintain interest rates at the recent Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting. According to Jin10, Logan emphasized the importance of assessing economic conditions before making any changes to the current monetary policy. The decision to keep rates unchanged reflects the Fed's cautious approach amid ongoing economic uncertainties. Logan's stance aligns with the broader consensus within the Fed to monitor inflation and employment data closely before considering any rate adjustments.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: Balance sheet can be reduced by changing regulatory rules.
data.description: Dallas Federal Reserve President Logan outlined the path and options for the Fed to shrink its balance sheet on Thursday, noting that the current system is functioning well and benefits overall financial stability. Logan stated that the Fed's current system for managing financial liquidity is designed to provide "ample" levels of reserves, and that the system is "efficient and effective." However, there are several ways to help reduce the Fed's holdings under the current system, many of which involve rules governing financial institutions' management of cash reserves. Recent internal and external research by the Fed indicates that by adjusting regulations to encourage banks to hold lower levels of reserves, the Fed could further shrink its balance sheet under the current system. Logan agreed, stating that the Fed is currently working to make reserve management "more efficient" during periods of stress. She also stated that some liquidity rules, while increasing reserves, have not improved safety because banks are reluctant to use these reserves during crises. "This is an inefficient use of the Fed's balance sheet, and we can completely avoid this," she said. (Jinshi)

data.title: CryptoQuant: Bitcoin may rebound to $71,500-$81,200 in the short term, but demand remains deeply contracted.
data.description: On-chain analytics firm CryptoQuant stated that Bitcoin demand remains in a "deep contraction" phase, with apparent demand decreasing by approximately 63,000 BTC in the 30 days leading up to the end of March, indicating that selling pressure still outweighs buying pressure. Although both ETFs and Michael Saylor's Strategy increased their buying, it was insufficient to alleviate overall pressure. ETFs saw 30-day buying of approximately 50,000 BTC, the highest since October 2025; Strategy's 30-day cumulative buying remained around 44,000 BTC. CryptoQuant pointed out that large holders ("whales" with 1,000–10,000 BTC) have turned net sellers, reducing their holdings by 188,000 BTC over the past year, confirming continued structural selling pressure. Mid-sized holders (the "dolphins" with 100–1,000 BTC) are still accumulating, but at a significantly slower pace, dropping from nearly 1 million BTC in October 2025 to 429,000 BTC. Furthermore, demand from US investors has weakened recently, with the Coinbase premium mostly negative, indicating that retail investors have not yet entered the market on a large scale. CryptoQuant suggests that if the macroeconomic environment improves, especially with a de-escalation of the US-Iran conflict, Bitcoin could rebound in the short term to the $71,500–$81,200 range, with $71,500 as initial resistance and $81,200 as the upper limit of the bear market rebound. (The Block)

data.title: Blockchain Technology Gains Traction Amid Technological Shifts
data.description:
Ethereum France leader Jerome de Tychey delivered the closing speech at EthCC[9], highlighting the transition of blockchain technology from skepticism to widespread adoption driven by efficiency and security. According to Foresight News, de Tychey emphasized that blockchain is at the intersection of technological changes such as AI and quantum-resistant encryption, positioning it as a core focus for the next 20 years.

Additionally, Jerome de Tychey announced that the next EthCC will be held from April 12 to 15, 2027, in Cannes, France.

data.title: Federal Reserve Considers Options for Reducing Balance Sheet
data.description: Federal Reserve officials are exploring various options for reducing the size of the central bank's balance sheet. According to ChainCatcher, Logan, a Federal Reserve official, stated that there are multiple interconnected strategies available for this process.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan Comments on Balance Sheet Expansion
data.description: The Federal Reserve's Logan has stated that expanding the Fed's balance sheet is acceptable if it meets public demand. According to ChainCatcher, this comment highlights the central bank's approach to managing its assets in response to economic needs. Logan's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the role of the Federal Reserve in supporting the economy through monetary policy adjustments.
